---
title: "Conclusion, GLOA 101"
author: "Byunghwan Son <br> George Mason University"
date: " "
#########################################
linkcolor: red
citecolor: blue
urlcolor: blueß
output: 
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    seal: false
    nature:
      ratio: '16:9'
      highlightStyle: github
      mathjax: "default"  
  #    self-contained: TRUE
  chakra: css/libs/remark-latest.min.js
css: ["default", 
      "css/footer.css", 
      "css/color.css", 
      # "css/xaringan-themer.css",
      default-fonts]
lib_dir: css/libs
beforeInit: "css/macros.js"
# seal: false  
#bibliography: /Users/bson3/Dropbox/latex/mybib2.bib
#remotes::install_github('yihui/xaringan', upgrade = TRUE)

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# to use FontAwesome
#htmltools::tagList(rmarkdown::html_dependency_font_awesome())
library(fs)
library(fontawesome)
library(vdemdata)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(WDI)
# library(magick)  
library(ggplot2)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, error=F, echo=F)
options(htmltools.preserve.raw = FALSE)
```

class: inverse, center, middle
background-image: url(https://media.tenor.com/QvDDT72U7lUAAAAM/crystal-ball.gif)
background-size: 100%

# .Huge[**The Conclusion**]

# .yellow[**The Future of Globalization**]

# .Large[.orange[**GLOA 101**]]

---

class: inverse, center, middle
background-image: url(https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/background_rightEmpty.jpg)
background-size: 100%

# .Large[.times[**Definition of globalization?**]]

--

# .Large[.red[increasing] and .blue[intensifying]]

--

# .Large[.Green[connectedness] between]

--

# .Large[global actors.]

---
class: inverse, center, middle
background-image: url(https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/night_background8.jpg)
background-size: 100%

# First, important to see if connectedness is shrinking.

```{r include=F, cache=T}

library(WDI)

var = c('GDPpc' = 'NY.GDP.PCAP.KN',
        'trade' = 'TG.VAL.TOTL.GD.ZS',
        'tariff' = 'TM.TAX.MRCH.SM.AR.ZS',
        'tourism' = 'ST.INT.TVLR.CD',
        'migrantStock' = 'SM.POP.TOTL',
        'trade_all' = 'NE.TRD.GNFS.ZS'
)

wdi <- WDI(indicator=var,
           start=1970,
           country = 'all',
           end=2021,
           extra=TRUE)   %>%
  filter(region !="Aggregates") %>%
  mutate(developed = 
           case_when(
             income == "High income" ~ "Developed",
             income == "Upper middle income" ~ "Developed",
             income == "Lower middle income" ~ "Underdeveloped",
             income == "Low income" ~ "Underdeveloped", 
             TRUE ~ NA_character_
           )) %>%
    filter(!is.na(developed))

```

---

# Has the volume of .red[trade] been shrinking? <br> .tiny[Merchandise Trade as % of GDP]

```{r results="asis", fig.width=16, fig.height=7}

wdi %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=year,
             y=trade)) +
  geom_point(# aes(color=developed),
             size=0.5) +
  geom_smooth(# aes(color=developed), 
              size=2, alpha=0.5, se=F) +
#  geom_smooth(aes(color='orange'), size=2, se=FALSE, linetype='dashed') +
  labs(x = "YEAR",
       y = "All Trade % of GDP") +
  theme_minimal() +
  # scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "orange", "blue" ),
  #                    labels = c("developed", "Overall", "Under-developed")) +
  theme(legend.title=element_text(size=0),
        legend.text=element_text(size=25),
        axis.text=element_text(size=20),
        axis.title=element_text(size=20)) +
  ylim(12, 25)

```

---

# .orange[Tariff?] .green[Tourism?]

--

```{r results="asis", fig.width=16, fig.height=7}

fig1 <- wdi %>% filter(year>1993) %>%
  filter(tariff<50) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=year,
             y=tariff,
             group=developed)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=developed),
             alpha=0.25, size=2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color=developed), 
              alpha=0.5) +
  labs(x = "YEAR",
       y = "Tariff Rates (outliers dropped)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title=element_text(size=0),
        legend.text=element_text(size=25),
        axis.text=element_text(size=20),
        axis.title=element_text(size=20),
        legend.position = 'bottom') 

fig2 <- wdi %>% filter(year>1993) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=year,
             y=log(tourism),
             group=developed)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=developed),
             alpha=0.25, size=2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color=developed), 
              alpha=0.5) +
  labs(x = "YEAR",
       y = "Tourism (receipts for travel items)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title=element_text(size=0),
        legend.text=element_text(size=25),
        axis.text=element_text(size=20),
        axis.title=element_text(size=20),
        legend.position='bottom') 

```

.pull-left[
```{r}

fig1

```
]

--

.pull-right[
```{r}

fig2

```
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle
background-image: url(https://media.tenor.com/AWDoghQs_xwAAAAM/magma-lava.gif)
background-size: 110%

# .LARGE[But .yellow[some] things are brewing.]

---

.full-width-green[
# What's happening? .yellow[Transitional] and uncertain phase
]
<br><br>
--

.LARGE[`r fa('flag', fill='red')` Long-term structural trends]
- .Large[Rise of China and relative U.S. decline (but .red[no] 'takeover')]
- .Large[Democratic backsliding & populism; anti-globalization backlash]

--

.LARGE[`r fa('flag', fill='purple')` Short-term foreign policy shocks] 
- .Large[Reduced reliability of U.S. leadership and alliances; ally-hedge]
-	.Large[More transactional and unilateral foreign policy]
-	.Large[Expanded use of economic coercion (sanctions)]

--

.LARGE[`r fa('flag', fill='green')` Tech and Info Transformation]
- .Large[Rise of AI and platform firms: implications for 'power']
- .Large[Spread of misinformation and “information disorder”]
	
---

.full-width-brown[
# What .yellow[will] happen? .tiny[(Leeds et al. 2025)]
]
<br>
--

- .LARGE[`r fa("scroll", fill="blue")`1: Transformation / Fragmentation]
	-	.Large[Emergence of rival blocs or “cliques”]
	-	.Large[Breakdown of shared norms]
	-	.Large[Competing governance systems (e.g., tech alliances, financial systems)]
	
--

- .LARGE[`r fa("scroll", fill="#80BFF2")`2: Adaptation / “Muddling Through”]
	-	.Large[Institutions persist but change functionally]
	-	.Large[Hybrid forms of governance emerge]
	
--

- .LARGE[`r fa("scroll", fill="#B2D8F7")`3: Continuity / Resilience: Some systems remain robust]
	-	.Large[Global trade governance]
	-	.Large[Territorial stability (limited return of conquest)]
	-	.Large[Persistence of some liberal practices even in autocracies]

---
class: inverse
background-image: url(https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/background_leftEmpty3.jpeg)
background-size: 110%


# Hard to predict because: (Steger, ch. 8)

--

## `r fa("angles-up", fill='#D980F2')` 'Disjuctive globalization' :
- .Large[different speeds/directions of changes across sectors]
- .Large[`r fa("robot", fill='yellow')` 'disembodied (digital/AI) globalization'  taking the lead]

--

## `r fa("circle-nodes", fill='#80BFF2')`  'Digital migration': 
- .Large[physically dispersed / digitally coordinated]

--

## 'Reglobalization'?

---

class: inverse, center, top
background-image: url(https://i0.kym-cdn.com/photos/images/newsfeed/000/960/452/98a.gif)
background-size: 110%

# .LARGE[**That concludes .Green[GLOA 101 lectures]. <br> See you at the review session.**]

---

background-image: url(https://y.yarn.co/210443c4-e455-4c8d-a2b0-46e562e411bb_text.gif)
background-size: 100%
